headline: Eli Lilly makes surprising retreat from major market
summary: Eli Lilly's (LLY) chairman and CEO Dave Ricks told German business newspaper Handelsblatt that Lilly would halve its planned investment in Germany, cutting its original 2.3 billion euro ($2.67 billion) commitment by roughly 50%. Reuters reported the announcement on June 3, 2026. The pullback lands ...

headline: Eli Lilly shares phase 3 data on triple agonist weight loss drug retatrutide
summary: Investing.com -- Eli Lilly and Company announced additional positive results today from pivotal Phase 3 trials of its investigational weight loss drug, retatrutide. The data, presented at the American Diabetes Association Scientific Sessions, positions the triple hormone receptor agonist as a potential next-generation successor to current dual-agonist therapies like tirzepatide. headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Eli Lilly (LLY) After Its 48% One-Year Surge?
summary: For investors wondering if Eli Lilly's share price still makes sense after such strong gains, or if expectations have run ahead of reality, this article focuses squarely on what you are paying versus what you may be getting. The stock last closed at US$1,131.42, with returns of 2.4% over 7 days, 14.6% over 30 days, 4.7% year to date and 48.0% over the past year, alongside a very large 3-year return and around 5x over 5 years. Recent market attention on Eli Lilly has largely centered on its...

headline: Lilly's triple agonist, retatrutide, drove substantial improvements in weight, A1C, knee osteoarthritis pain, and obstructive sleep apnea, demonstrating its remarkable potential to treat obesity and its complications

headline: Pfizer targets obesity market with monthly weight-loss injection data
summary: Investing.com -- Pfizer Inc (NYSE:PFE) has unveiled detailed strategy plans and clinical data for its experimental weight-loss injection, aiming to secure a foothold in a competitive market currently dominated by rivals. The New York-based pharmaceutical giant is working to catch up to industry leaders Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE:LLY) and Novo Nordisk A/S (NYSE:NVO), which have captured significant market share through their first-mover advantages. headline: Eli Lilly Broadens Beyond Obesity Drugs With Vaccine And Genetic Bets
summary: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is pushing beyond its obesity drug franchise with plans to acquire three vaccine focused biotech companies. The company has also entered new collaborations in genetic and RNA based therapies with partners including Ascidian Therapeutics, Hanmi Pharm, and Haisco Pharmaceutical Group. These moves come alongside broader offerings on LillyDirect, signaling a wider repositioning of its product pipeline. Eli Lilly, trading at $1,131.42, has seen very large share price gains...
